A planet is said to be defeated when it is situated to the south
of the other, is rough, quivering, retreating without actually com-
ing in contact with the other, small, mounted upon, of unnatural
appearance, without brilliance and colourless,
[Here nine marks of defeat are listed, but Pflraiara has one
more, viz. Abhihata or struck.

A planet possessed of characteristics contrary to those mention-
ed above should be considered as victorious i.e. when it is large,
glossy and brilliant, even though it may be placed in the south,
